Class JdbcOutbox

java.lang.Object
io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.jdbc.JdbcOutbox
All Implemented Interfaces:
Outbox
public final class JdbcOutbox extends Object implements Outbox
JDBC implementation of Outbox. Writes one row per OutboxEvent to the configured outbox table within the caller's active transaction.

JdbcOutbox is database-agnostic: every database-specific decision (idempotency clause, JSON column binding, timestamp binding, SQLState classification) is delegated to an OutboxDialect. The dialect is either supplied explicitly via JdbcOutbox.Builder.dialect(OutboxDialect) or auto-detected on first publish through OutboxDialectProvider (registered via ServiceLoader).

The class is thread-safe; the dialect is resolved lazily under a double-checked volatile field so the hot path stays lock-free after the first publish. Every publish/publishAll call asks the dialect for a fresh OutboxInsert handle against the caller-supplied Connection. JdbcOutbox never closes the connection: that is the caller's responsibility (Spring's transaction manager, the caller's try-with-resources block, etc.).

Construct via builder().

Since:
0.1.0

  • Method Details

    • builder

      public static JdbcOutbox.Builder builder()
      Creates a fresh JdbcOutbox.Builder.
      Returns:
      a new builder.
      Since:
      0.1.0

    • publish

      public void publish(OutboxEvent event)
      Description copied from interface: Outbox
      Persists the given event to the outbox table.

      The write is performed inside the caller's active transaction and is rolled back together with the caller's business writes if that transaction aborts.

      Specified by:
      publish in interface Outbox
      Parameters:
      event - the event to persist; never null.

    • publishAll

      public void publishAll(Iterable<OutboxEvent> events)
      Description copied from interface: Outbox
      Persists every event in events to the outbox table, atomically with respect to the caller's transaction.

      The default implementation simply iterates and calls Outbox.publish(OutboxEvent) per event. Implementations are encouraged to override this with a true batch write (e.g. JDBC addBatch / executeBatch) when the underlying store supports it. The default exists for source compatibility — every Outbox produced before this method was added still satisfies the contract.

      If any individual write fails, the surrounding transaction will not be committed by this library; the failure surfaces as an OutboxException subtype on the offending event.

      Specified by:
      publishAll in interface Outbox
      Parameters:
      events - the events to persist; never null, individual elements must not be null.
